name: Light Hammer
description: This smaller version of the warhammer has a wooden or metal shaft ending in a metal head. Unlike its heavier cousin, it is light enough to throw. Cold iron weapons deal additional damage to creatures with weakness to cold iron, like demons and fey.

name: Longsword
description: Longswords can be one-edged or two-edged swords. Their blades are heavy and they're between 3 and 4 feet in length. Cold iron weapons deal additional damage to creatures with weakness to cold iron, like demons and fey.

name: Pick
description: A pick designed solely for combat has a sturdy wooden shaft and a heavy, pointed head to deliver devastating blows.

name: Acid Flask
description: A flask of corrosive acid that eats through flesh and material. Deals 1 acid on a direct hit, 1d6 persistent acid, and 1 acid splash.

strikes.name: Acid Flask
strikes.description: Deals 1 acid on a direct hit, 1d6 persistent acid, and 1 acid splash.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Acid Flask (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1 acid, 1d6 persistent acid, 1 splash

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Acid Flask (Moderate)
variants.effect: 1 acid, 2d6 persistent acid, 2 splash

strikes.name: Acid Flask
strikes.description: Deals 1 acid on a direct hit, 2d6 persistent acid, and 2 acid splash.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Acid Flask (Greater)
variants.effect: 1 acid, 3d6 persistent acid, 3 splash

strikes.name: Acid Flask
strikes.description: Deals 1 acid on a direct hit, 3d6 persistent acid, and 3 acid splash.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Acid Flask (Major)
variants.effect: 1 acid, 4d6 persistent acid, 4 splash

strikes.name: Acid Flask
strikes.description: Deals 1 acid on a direct hit, 4d6 persistent acid, and 4 acid splash.

name: Aeon Stone (Agate Ellipsoid)
description: This banded agate orbits your head. You can cast augury as a 2nd-rank divine innate spell once per day. Resonant Power The augury spell from this aeon stone always succeeds at its DC 6 flat check.

name: Aeon Stone (Azure Briolette)
description: This deep blue, teardrop-cut gem orbits your head. Whenever an enemy fails a save against one of your mental spells, you gain temporary Hit Points equal to twice the spell's rank until the start of your next turn. Resonant Power You can cast chill touch as an occult innate spell once per day using a two-action activation.

name: Aeon Stone (Clear Quartz Octagon)
description: This clear, octagonal stone orbits your head. When a non-magical item in your possession worth 90 gp or less and of 1 Bulk or less would become broken, this aeon stone automatically activates, preventing the item from breaking. The aeon stone then becomes consumed. Resonant Power You can cast mending as a 1st-rank arcane innate spell once per day.

name: Aeon Stone (Clear Spindle)
description: This clear, spindle-shaped stone orbits your head. You don't need to eat or drink while it's invested by you. It doesn't function until it has been worn continuously for a week and invested each day therein; if invested by someone else, this interval starts over. Resonant Power You can cast air bubble as a primal innate spell once per day.

name: Aeon Stone (Consumed)
description: This dull lump has lost its magical properties. It still rotates around your head like any other aeon stone, and can thus serve as a stylish, hands-free option for spells that target an object. This aeon stone has no resonant power.

name: Aeon Stone (Dusty Rose Prism)
description: This faintly glowing pink prism orbits your head. While the stone is invested, you can cast the shield cantrip as a 1st-rank arcane innate spell, conjuring a barrier of pink energy. Resonant Power The resonant power increases the damage prevented by your aeon stone's shield spell from 5 to 10.

name: Aeon Stone (Formulating)
description: This faceted stone orbits your head and contains the formulas of a basic crafter's book; while you have this knowledge, you can write the formulas down for later use. You gain a +1 item bonus to checks made to Craft. Resonant Power You can cast phantasmal minion as an occult innate spell once per day.

name: Aeon Stone (Gold Nodule)
description: This gold nodule orbits your head when invested. When the stone is created, its creator chooses a language they know to store within it; while you invest the stone, you can understand, speak, and write that language. Resonant Power You can cast comprehend language as an occult innate spell once per day.

name: Aeon Stone (Pale Lavender Ellipsoid)
description: This pale lavender, ellipsoid stone orbits your head. It must be activated (reaction, when a spell targets you or includes you in its area) to cast a 6th-rank dispel magic that attempts to counteract the triggering spell (counteract modifier +22); it then becomes consumed. Resonant Power You can cast the read aura cantrip as an arcane innate spell.

name: Aeon Stone (Pearly White Spindle)
description: This pale, spindle-shaped stone orbits your head. While it's invested, it slowly heals your wounds, restoring 1 Hit Point every minute. Resonant Power You gain resistance 1 to void damage.

name: Aeon Stone (Polished Pebble)
description: This smooth, river-worn pebble orbits your head. While invested, you gain a +1 item bonus to saves and DCs against attempts to Grapple or Swallow you. Resonant Power You can cast grease as a primal innate spell once per day, but only to target a surface.

name: Aeon Stone (Preserving)
description: This translucent stone orbits your head. You gain resistance 3 to persistent damage. At the end of any turn when persistent damage you're taking can't overcome this resistance, that persistent damage ends. Resonant Power You can cast stabilize as a divine innate spell.

name: Aeon Stone (Repairing)
description: This polished stone orbits your head. You gain a +1 item bonus to checks made to Craft, and when you Repair an item you restore an additional 5 Hit Points. Resonant Power You can cast mending as an arcane innate spell once per day.

name: Aether Marbles
description: A handful of crystalline marbles that shatter into eddies of raw force. Deals 2d6 force and 2 force splash; the splash area fills with twisting eddies of force that are difficult terrain for incorporeal creatures (but not corporeal ones).

strikes.name: Aether Marbles
strikes.description: Deals 2d6 force and 2 force splash; the splash area fills with twisting eddies of force that are difficult terrain for incorporeal creatures (but not corporeal ones).

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Aether Marbles (Lesser)
variants.effect: 2d6 force, 2 splash; difficult terrain (incorporeal)

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Aether Marbles (Moderate)
variants.effect: 3d6 force, 3 splash; difficult terrain (incorporeal)

strikes.name: Aether Marbles
strikes.description: Deals 3d6 force and 3 force splash; the splash area fills with twisting eddies of force that are difficult terrain for incorporeal creatures (but not corporeal ones).

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Aether Marbles (Greater)
variants.effect: 4d6 force, 4 splash; difficult terrain (incorporeal)

strikes.name: Aether Marbles
strikes.description: Deals 4d6 force and 4 force splash; the splash area fills with twisting eddies of force that are difficult terrain for incorporeal creatures (but not corporeal ones).

name: Aim-Aiding
description: Armor etched with this rune aids in routing ranged attacks aimed at an enemy around you. You don't provide enemies cover against your allies' ranged attacks.

name: Alchemist's Fire
description: A volatile concoction that bursts into flame on impact. Deals 1d8 fire, 1 persistent fire, and 1 fire splash.

strikes.name: Alchemist's Fire
strikes.description: Deals 1d8 fire, 1 persistent fire, and 1 fire splash.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Alchemist's Fire (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1d8 fire, 1 persistent fire, 1 splash

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Alchemist's Fire (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2d8 fire, 2 persistent fire, 2 splash

strikes.name: Alchemist's Fire
strikes.description: Deals 2d8 fire, 2 persistent fire, and 2 fire splash.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Alchemist's Fire (Greater)
variants.effect: 3d8 fire, 3 persistent fire, 3 splash

strikes.name: Alchemist's Fire
strikes.description: Deals 3d8 fire, 3 persistent fire, and 3 fire splash.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Alchemist's Fire (Major)
variants.effect: 4d8 fire, 4 persistent fire, 4 splash

strikes.name: Alchemist's Fire
strikes.description: Deals 4d8 fire, 4 persistent fire, and 4 fire splash.

actions.name: Shining Charge
actions.description: Frequency once per hour; Effect You evoke the spirit of the unicorn whose horn forms the lance and ride a shining charge: Stride up to twice your Speed and make a Strike with the alicorn lance, gaining the benefits of the lance's jousting trait on the Strike. If you're already mounted, the unicorn spirit takes shape around your steed instead, granting it a +10-foot status bonus to its Speed for the charge.

name: Alicorn Lance
description: This white +1 striking lance is carved from a unicorn's horn. In addition to its normal damage, it deals 1 spirit damage. You can use a single action (concentrate) to make it glow like a torch or to extinguish that light.

actions.name: Twin Venom Strike
actions.description: Frequency once per hour; Effect You make two unarmed Strikes. Both Strikes count toward your multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after you've made both attacks. Each Strike deals an additional 1d6 poison damage.

name: Amphisbaena Handwraps
description: These handwraps are made from amphisbaena skin, with venomous mouths that your hands fit through. Your unarmed strikes gain the versatile P trait. Amphisbaena handwraps can have weapon runes etched onto them, similar to handwraps of mighty blows.

name: Anticorrosion Oil
description: You can coat objects of 6 Bulk or less with anticorrosion oil. For 24 hours, the object takes half damage from acid and from all effects that specifically cause it to rust or corrode, such as contact with a rust monster's antennae.

consumable.label: Anticorrosion
consumable.note: Half damage from acid/rust (24 hr)

name: Antidote

variants.label: Lesser
variants.effect: You gain a +2 item bonus to Fortitude saving throws against poisons.

variants.label: Moderate
variants.effect: You gain a +3 item bonus to Fortitude saving throws against poisons.

description: An antidote protects you against toxins. Upon drinking an antidote, you gain an item bonus to Fortitude saving throws against poisons for 6 hours.

consumable.note: +2 item Fort vs poison (6 hr)

name: Antiplague

variants.label: Lesser
variants.effect: You gain a +2 item bonus to Fortitude saving throws against diseases.

variants.label: Moderate
variants.effect: You gain a +3 item bonus to Fortitude saving throws against diseases.

description: Antiplague can fortify the body defenses against diseases. Upon drinking an antiplague, you gain an item bonus to Fortitude saving throws against diseases for 24 hours; this applies to your daily save against a disease progression.

consumable.note: +2 item Fort vs disease (24 hr)

actions.name: Release Cantrip
actions.description: Activate (varies); Effect Activating an archaic wayfinder is the same as the casting for the relevant cantrip stored within it. Once it has been activated, it becomes empty again until another cantrip is cast into it.

name: Archaic Wayfinder
description: This antique compass acts as a repository for one cantrip cast into it. To fill an archaic wayfinder, a spellcaster casts the chosen cantrip into the empty device, providing any necessary cost; the spell can't be one that's normally cast as a free action or reaction. An archaic wayfinder can also hold an aeon stone and activate its resonant power, counting against the limit of one wayfinder with a slotted resonant aeon stone.

name: Armor Potency
description: Magic wards deflect attacks, increasing the armor's item bonus to AC. The armor can be etched with property runes up to the potency's value.

variants.name: Armor Potency (+1)
variants.description: Increase the armor's item bonus to AC by 1. The armor can be etched with one property rune.

variants.name: Armor Potency (+2)
variants.description: Increase the armor's item bonus to AC by 2, and the armor can be etched with two property runes.

variants.name: Armor Potency (+3)
variants.description: Increase the armor's item bonus to AC by 3, and the armor can be etched with three property runes.

actions.name: Cast Tailwind
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Duration 1 hour. The wind at your back pushes you to find new horizons. You gain a +10-foot status bonus to your Speed.

name: Avaria Tattoo
description: This tattoo features traditional Varisian geometry and is part of an astrological set of symbol tattoos. Having one provides you with a +1 to Diplomacy checks when speaking to native Varisians, and allows you to cast Tailwind once per day. (Usage: tattooed on the body.)

actions.name: Cast Vanishing Tracks
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Area 20-foot emanation; Duration 1 hour. The DC of checks to Track you and up to 10 creatures of your choice in the area gains a +4 status bonus, or is equal to your spell DC, whichever results in a higher DC.

name: Avidais Tattoo
description: This tattoo features traditional Varisian geometry and is part of an astrological set of symbol tattoos. Having one provides you with a +1 to Diplomacy checks when speaking to native Varisians, and allows you to cast Vanishing Tracks once per day. (Usage: tattooed on the body.)

name: Axe Musket
description: A sturdy musket with an axeblade attached near the muzzle, this combination weapon is favored by firearm-using dwarves and barbarians. As a combination weapon, you can attack with either its melee axe or its ranged firearm.

name: Backfire Mantle
description: This vivid red cloak of sturdy fabric is favored by aggressive battle alchemists and mages, as well as those cautious warriors who need to advance into the fray ahead of the blast-happy back line. The mantle interposes to protect you from your own and allies' magic, granting you a circumstance bonus to Reflex saves against your own spells, as well as those of your allies. You also gain resistance to splash damage from your own alchemical items and those of your allies.

variants.label: Standard
variants.name: Backfire Mantle
variants.effect: The mantle grants a +1 circumstance bonus to Reflex saves against your own and allies' spells, and resistance 3 to splash damage from your own and allies' alchemical items.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Backfire Mantle (Greater)
variants.effect: The mantle grants a +2 circumstance bonus to Reflex saves against your own and allies' spells, and resistance 10 to splash damage from your own and allies' alchemical items.

name: Backpack
description: A backpack for holding your gear

name: Bagpipes of Turmoil
description: These droning war-pipes wail with a discordant, unsettling skirl that stirs dread in those who hear it. While playing the bagpipes, you gain a +1 item bonus to Performance checks and to Intimidation checks to Demoralize. You can Activate the bagpipes to Cast a Spell from its staff spell list.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Bagpipes of Turmoil (Greater)
variants.effect: Adds 2nd- and 3rd-rank spells to the staff.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Bagpipes of Turmoil (Major)
variants.effect: The item bonus while playing increases to +2. Adds 4th- and 5th-rank spells to the staff.

name: Battle Axe
description: These axes are designed explicitly as weapons, rather than tools. They typically weigh less than tree-felling axes, with a shaft reinforced with metal bands and a sharper blade, making them ideal for chopping limbs rather than wood.

name: Beacon Shot
description: Ammunition (arrow or bolt); Activate (one-action, manipulate). The shaft of a beacon shot is studded with flecks of gemstones. When an activated beacon shot hits a target, it embeds itself into that target and spews sparks for 1 minute. If the target is invisible, it becomes merely hidden to creatures who would otherwise be unable to see it. The sparks also negate the concealed condition if the target was otherwise concealed. A creature can remove the arrow or bolt by using an Interact basic action and succeeding at a DC 20 Athletics check.

name: Bedroll
description: A compact mat, not too comfy but suitable for long journeys.

name: Belt
description: A sturdy leather belt.

actions.name: Draw a Blade
actions.description: Effect You pull a link from the armor, which transforms into a +1 striking dagger. The dagger disappears and reappears as a link in the armor after you Strike with it, or at the end of this turn if you don't make a Strike. You can Activate the blade byrnie in place of an Interact action to draw a weapon for abilities such as the Quick Draw feat.

name: Blade Byrnie
description:
Instead of chain links, this +1 chain shirt is assembled from metal "leaves" that each resemble a small blade.

Upgrading the runes on the blade byrnie makes the daggers pulled from it more powerful. The daggers have a +2 weapon potency rune if the armor has a +2 armor potency rune, or a +3 weapon potency rune if the armor has a +3 armor potency rune.

name: Blight Bomb
description: A bomb of concentrated toxins that sickens on contact. Deals 1d4 poison, 1d4 persistent poison, and 1 poison splash.

strikes.name: Blight Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 1d4 poison, 1d4 persistent poison, and 1 poison splash.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Blight Bomb (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1d4 poison, 1d4 persistent poison, 1 splash

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Blight Bomb (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2d4 poison, 2d4 persistent poison, 2 splash

strikes.name: Blight Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 2d4 poison, 2d4 persistent poison, and 2 poison splash.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Blight Bomb (Greater)
variants.effect: 3d4 poison, 3d4 persistent poison, 3 splash

strikes.name: Blight Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 3d4 poison, 3d4 persistent poison, and 3 poison splash.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Blight Bomb (Major)
variants.effect: 4d4 poison, 4d4 persistent poison, 4 splash

strikes.name: Blight Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 4d4 poison, 4d4 persistent poison, and 4 poison splash.

name: Blindpepper Bomb
description: A burst of caustic pepper dust that blinds those caught in the cloud. Thrown to a range of 30 feet, it bursts in a 15-foot radius; each creature in the area must attempt a DC 18 Reflex save. On a failure the creature is blinded for 1 round, then dazzled for 1 round; on a critical failure it is blinded for 1 round, sickened 1, and dazzled until the sickened condition ends.

name: Blood Booster
description: This elixir bolsters your body's natural defenses and ability to resist maladies that travel through or affect blood. For 10 minutes you receive the listed resistance to persistent bleed and persistent poison damage, and you lower the DC for any flat checks to end persistent bleed or persistent poison damage as if you received particularly appropriate aid. At the GM's discretion, blood booster elixirs can also automatically counteract non-magical effects that specifically rely on thinning the drinker's blood. Activate (1 action, manipulate).

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Blood Booster (Lesser)
variants.effect: The resistance is 5.

consumable.note: Resistance 5 to persistent bleed & poison (10 min)

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Blood Booster (Moderate)
variants.effect: The resistance is 10.

consumable.note: Resistance 10 to persistent bleed & poison (10 min)

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Blood Booster (Greater)
variants.effect: The resistance is 20.

consumable.note: Resistance 20 to persistent bleed & poison (10 min)

name: Bloodseeker Beak

variants.label: Standard
variants.effect: Free action when you hit an off-guard creature with the affixed weapon: deal an extra 1d4 precision damage. If you deal sneak attack damage to the creature, it also takes 1d4 persistent bleed damage.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Bloodseeker Beak (Greater)
variants.effect: As standard, but 2d4 precision damage and 2d4 persistent bleed damage.

description: A talisman shaped like a mosquito's proboscis, affixed to a weapon. When you hit an off-guard creature, activate it to draw extra blood.

consumable.note: +1d4 precision (2d4 greater); +bleed vs sneak-attacked targets

name: Boots
description: Sturdy leather footwear, made for long roads.

name: Boots of Bounding

variants.label: Standard
variants.effect: You gain a +5-foot item bonus to your Speed and a +2 item bonus to Athletics checks to High Jump and Long Jump. When you Leap, move 5 feet farther horizontally or 3 feet higher vertically.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Boots of Bounding (Greater)
variants.effect: The Speed bonus is +10 feet and the High Jump / Long Jump bonus is +3.

description: These springy boots lend a bounding lightness to every stride and leap.

name: Bottled Lightning
description: A jar of crackling captured lightning. Deals 1d6 electricity and 1 electricity splash; on a hit the target is off-guard until the start of your next turn.

strikes.name: Bottled Lightning
strikes.description: Deals 1d6 electricity and 1 electricity splash; on a hit the target is off-guard until the start of your next turn.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Bottled Lightning (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1d6 electricity, 1 splash; off-guard on hit

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Bottled Lightning (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2d6 electricity, 2 splash; off-guard on hit

strikes.name: Bottled Lightning
strikes.description: Deals 2d6 electricity and 2 electricity splash; on a hit the target is off-guard until the start of your next turn.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Bottled Lightning (Greater)
variants.effect: 3d6 electricity, 3 splash; off-guard on hit

strikes.name: Bottled Lightning
strikes.description: Deals 3d6 electricity and 3 electricity splash; on a hit the target is off-guard until the start of your next turn.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Bottled Lightning (Major)
variants.effect: 4d6 electricity, 4 splash; off-guard on hit

strikes.name: Bottled Lightning
strikes.description: Deals 4d6 electricity and 4 electricity splash; on a hit the target is off-guard until the start of your next turn.

actions.name: Jangling Dash
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect You gain a +10-foot status bonus to your Speed for 1 minute.

name: Bracelet of Dashing
description: This jangling, silvery bracelet makes you lighter on your feet, giving you a +1 item bonus to Acrobatics checks.

name: Bracers of Missile Deflection

variants.label: Standard
variants.effect: Reaction, once per day, when a ranged weapon attack hits you but doesn't critically hit and you're aware of it and not off-guard: gain a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack; if this turns the hit into a failure, the attack misses you.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Bracers of Missile Deflection (Greater)
variants.effect: As standard, but usable once every 10 minutes.

description: A pair of reinforced bracers that let you knock an incoming missile off course.

name: Breastplate
description: Though referred to as a breastplate, this type of armor consists of several pieces of plate or half-plate armor that protect the torso, chest, neck, and sometimes the hips and lower legs. It strategically grants some of the protection of plate while allowing greater flexibility and speed.

name: Brooch of Shielding
description: This piece of silver or gold jewelry, adorned with miniature images of kite shields, can be used to fasten a cloak or cape. The brooch automatically absorbs magic missile spells targeting you. A brooch of shielding can absorb 30 individual magic missiles before it melts and becomes useless. Sometimes when found, a brooch of shielding has already absorbed a number of missiles.

name: Buckler
description: This very small shield is a favorite of duelists and quick, lithe warriors. It's typically made of steel and strapped to your forearm. You can Raise a Shield with your buckler as long as you have that hand free or are holding a light object that's not a weapon in that hand.

name: Cape
description: A shoulder-fastened cape.

name: Cape of Illumination
description: This golden cape, embroidered with vibrant red sun rays, sparkles like sunlight reflecting off the ocean.

actions.name: Shed Light
actions.description: Activate command, Interact; Effect The cape glows with the effects of a 1st-rank light spell (4th-rank for Moderate, 7th-rank for Greater). You can activate it again to Dismiss the effect.

actions.name: Blinding Flash
actions.description: Activate envision, Interact; Frequency once per day (once per hour for Greater); Effect The cape unleashes a blinding flash. Each enemy within 30 feet that can see you must attempt a DC 19 Fortitude save (DC 25 for Moderate, DC 34 for Greater). Critical Success Unaffected. Success Dazzled 1 round. Failure Blinded 1 round and dazzled 1 minute (the creature can use an Interact action to end the blinded condition). Critical Failure Blinded 1 round and dazzled 1 hour.

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Cape of Illumination (Moderate)
variants.effect: The constant light is a 4th-rank light spell, and the blinding flash DC is 25.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Cape of Illumination (Greater)
variants.effect: The constant light is a 7th-rank light spell. The blinding flash DC is 34, and it can be activated once per hour.

actions.name: Cast Liberating Command
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Range 60 feet; Targets 1 creature. You call out a liberating cry, urging an ally to break free of an effect that holds them in place. If the target is grabbed, immobilized, or restrained, it can immediately use a reaction to attempt to Escape.

name: Carnasia Tattoo
description: This tattoo features traditional Varisian geometry and is part of an astrological set of symbol tattoos. Having one provides you with a +1 to Diplomacy checks when speaking to native Varisians, and allows you to cast Liberating Command once per day. (Usage: tattooed on the body.)

name: Caterwaul Sling
description: Made of shiny brown leather, this +1 striking sling has a single white thread interwoven into its cord.

actions.name: Unleash Roar
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect You pull the white thread free and whirl the sling at high speed, letting out an ear-piercing wave of sound. Each creature in a 30-foot cone takes 4d6 sonic damage (DC 21 basic Fortitude save). On a failure the creature is also deafened for 1 round; on a critical failure, deafened for 1 hour.

name: Chain Mail
description: A suit of chainmail consists of several pieces of armor composed of small metal rings linked together in a protective mesh. It typically includes a chain shirt, leggings, a pair of arms, and a coif, covering most of the body.

name: Chain Shirt
description: Sometimes called a hauberk, this is a long shirt constructed of the same metal rings as chainmail. However, it is much lighter than chainmail and protects only the torso, upper arms, and upper legs of its wearer.

name: Chalk
description: Suitable for marking hard surfaces temporarily.

name: Channel Protection Amulet
description: This nugget of polished tektite is trapped in a cage of braided wire and hangs from a silken cord. When wearing this amulet, you gain resistance 5 against damage from harm spells if you're living, or against heal spells if you're undead.

name: Circlet of Persuasion
description: This elegant circlet is worn around the head. While wearing it, you gain a +1 item bonus to Charisma-based skill checks.

name: Clandestine Cloak
description:
When you pull up the hood of this nondescript gray cloak (an Interact action), you become drab and uninteresting: you gain a +1 item bonus to Stealth checks and to Deception checks to Impersonate a forgettable background character, but take a -1 item penalty to Diplomacy and Intimidation checks. Toggle the hood mode to apply these while the hood is up.

Activate—Cloak Identity (two actions, concentrate, manipulate; once per day): You pull the hood up and gain the benefits of veil of privacy for 1 hour or until you pull the hood back down.

name: Cloak
description: A warm hooded traveler's cloak.

name: Cloak of Elvenkind

variants.label: Standard
variants.effect: Adjust the clasp (Interact) and the cloak matches your surroundings and muffles your sounds, granting a +1 item bonus to Stealth checks. Activate (single action, once per day) to draw the hood up and gain the effects of invisibility until you lower the hood (twice per day if you also wear boots of elvenkind).

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Cloak of Elvenkind (Greater)
variants.effect: The Stealth bonus is +2, and the hood grants 4th-rank invisibility. With boots of elvenkind, you can Sneak in forests even while observed.

description: A green-gray cloak woven with elven artistry that blends into the wilds and hushes your footfalls.

name: Cloak of Feline Rest
description: This black velvet cloak is featureless and very soft to the touch. Upon wearing it for the first time, you're momentarily overwhelmed with a sense of comfort and coziness. While wearing this cloak you can comfortably rest in any space, so long as it's not wet or particularly hazardous. While sleeping in this cloak you only take a –2 status penalty to auditory Perception checks, rather than a –4 status penalty.

name: Cloak of Repute
description: This gaudy and extravagant cloak is adorned with brilliant gems and a fur trim. While wearing the cloak, you gain instant fame and notoriety, granting you a +1 item bonus to Diplomacy checks to Make an Impression. Creatures attempting to learn about you gain the same bonus to Gather Information about you.

actions.name: Make a Request
actions.description: Activate command; Frequency once per day; Effect You wield your great stature to make a Request of a creature. If you roll a success on the check, you get a critical success instead.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Cloak of Repute (Greater)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +2. You can activate the cloak twice per day.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Cloak of Repute (Major)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +3. You can activate the cloak three times per day. If you roll a critical failure on your Diplomacy check when you activate the cloak, you get a failure instead.

name: Cloak of the Bat
description: A supple, leathery cloak. While wearing it you gain a +2 item bonus to Stealth checks and to Acrobatics checks to Maneuver in Flight, and you can hang upside down from any surface that supports your weight without a check. Activate (two actions, command, Interact; once per day) to either transform the cloak into membranous wings that grant you a fly Speed of 30 feet for 10 minutes, or turn yourself into a bat as a 4th-rank pest form spell. A draconic monk can flavor the wings as scaled dragon wings.

name: Club
description: This is a simple piece of stout wood shaped or repurposed to bludgeon an enemy. Clubs can be intricately carved pieces of martial art or as simple as a tree branch or piece of wood. It is light enough to throw.

name: Codex of Unimpeded Sight
description: A female figure peers through her blindfold from the cover of this stately tome. It can be used as a grimoire for prepared arcane spellcasting.

actions.name: Unimpeded Sight
actions.description: Activate (free action, envision); Frequency once per day; Requirements Your last action was to cast a divination spell prepared from this grimoire; Effect The grimoire offers you a glimpse into the truth of things. You Seek or Recall Knowledge.

name: Cognitive Mutagen
description: An alchemical mutagen that sharpens the intellect at the cost of physical prowess.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Cognitive Mutagen (Lesser)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +1.

consumable.note: +1 mental/RK skills; −2 attacks, Athletics, Acrobatics (1 min)

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Cognitive Mutagen (Moderate)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +2.

consumable.note: +2 mental/RK skills; −2 attacks, Athletics, Acrobatics (10 min)

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Cognitive Mutagen (Greater)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +3.

consumable.note: +3 mental/RK skills; −2 attacks, Athletics, Acrobatics (1 hr)

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Cognitive Mutagen (Major)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +4.

consumable.note: +4 mental/RK skills; −2 attacks, Athletics, Acrobatics (1 hr)

name: Corpseward Pendant
description: This worn amulet is usually shaped like a humanoid skull or small animal skull, warding its wearer from the notice of the restless dead.

actions.name: Hide from the Dead
actions.description: Activate Interact; Frequency three times per day; Effect You become undetectable to undead creatures for 10 minutes. Undead can't see, hear, or smell you, nor detect you with senses such as scent or tremorsense. A creature can attempt a single DC 18 Will save to ignore this effect. If an undead has reason to believe an undetected opponent is present, it can still attempt to Seek or Strike you.

name: Crafter's Eyepiece
description: This rugged metal eyepiece etched with square patterns is designed to be worn over a single eye. Twisting the lens reveals a faint three-dimensional outline of an item you plan to build or repair, with helpful labels on the component parts. You gain a +1 item bonus to Crafting checks. When you Repair an item, increase the Hit Points restored to 10 + 10 per proficiency rank on a success or 15 + 15 per proficiency rank on a critical success.

name: Crescent Cross
description: A crescent cross combines a small scizore with an arm-mounted crossbow apparatus that can hold up to three bolts at a time. It changes chambers automatically after you fire. To reload, you must load each bolt into the aparatus individually as its own action.

name: Crossbow
description: This ranged weapon has a bow-like assembly mounted on a handled frame called a tiller. The tiller has a mechanism to lock the bowstring in place, attached to a trigger mechanism that releases the tension and launches a bolt. Reloading takes 1 Interact action.

actions.name: Shield the Companion
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect You bow to an ally within 30 feet, casting shield other targeting the ally; the link remains even if you later move more than 30 feet away. At the end of the spell's duration, your ally recovers 4d8 Hit Points and you recover half of what they recover.

name: Crown of the Companion
description: This regal crown links you to an ally you protect. While wearing it, you gain a +1 item bonus to Diplomacy checks.

name: Cultist Cowl
description: The fabric of a cultist cowl is either raspy burlap, durable cotton, or fine silk, depending on the item's power. When a character who worships a single deity dons a cultist cowl, the cowl's colors change to match those sacred to that deity, and the deity's symbol appears on the front. While worn, you gain a +1 item bonus to Religion checks. You also gain a +1 item bonus to all skill checks attempted to aid a ritual by being a secondary caster.

name: Dagger
description: This small, bladed weapon is held in one hand and used to stab a creature in close combat. It can also be thrown.

name: Devil's Breath Incense
description: This 6-inch stick of gray-green incense has an unsettling, oily look. Activate (Interact): When burned, it emits a brimstone-like stink and burns for 10 minutes. A creature within 10 feet must succeed at a Fortitude save or become sickened 1 (sickened 2 on a critical failure), after which it is temporarily immune for 1 hour. While the stick burns, an adjacent creature can inhale forcefully (Interact), consuming the entire stick and becoming sickened 3; on its next turn it can exhale a 15-foot cone of foul-smelling smoke that sickens creatures in the area.

actions.name: Diplomat's Bearing
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect Attempt a DC 20 check to Recall Knowledge about people of a human ethnicity, a non-human ancestry, or some other type of creature (the GM determines your options). If you succeed, the badge's bonus increases to +2 for Diplomacy checks with creatures of that group for the rest of the day.

name: Diplomat's Badge
description: When displayed prominently, this brass badge makes creatures find you more agreeable. You gain a +1 item bonus to Diplomacy checks.

name: Dog-Bone Knife
description: The blade of this +1 striking dagger is made from the thigh bone of a canine that died protecting its master. A willing polymorphed creature or werecreature that takes damage from the knife must succeed at a DC 19 Will save or immediately revert to its natural form; on a success it is temporarily immune for 1 minute. Creatures vulnerable to canines (such as kushtakas) take a -2 circumstance penalty to the save, and the knife ignores their resistance to physical damage. Unwilling polymorph victims are unaffected.

name: Doubling Rings
description: This item consists of two magically linked rings: an intricate, gleaming golden ring with a square-cut ruby, and a thick, plain iron ring. When you wield a melee weapon in the hand wearing the golden ring, the weapon's fundamental runes (weapon potency and striking) are replicated onto any melee weapon you wield in the hand wearing the iron ring; any fundamental runes on the iron-ring weapon are suppressed. The replication functions only while you wear both rings and wield a melee weapon in each hand, so it doesn't apply to thrown attacks or two-handed weapons held in one hand.

variants.label: Standard
variants.name: Doubling Rings
variants.effect: Replicates the fundamental runes (potency and striking) from the gold-ring weapon onto the iron-ring weapon.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Doubling Rings (Greater)
variants.effect: The rings also replicate property runes from the gold-ring weapon, so long as the iron-ring weapon meets all the prerequisites for a given rune and is not a specific weapon. All the iron-ring weapon's own runes are suppressed. When you invest the rings, you can elect to transfer only fundamental runes, functioning as standard doubling rings.

reactions.name: Roll the Plates
reactions.trigger: A creature adjacent to you targets you with a melee attack.
reactions.description: You roll the hefty plates of the dragon turtle armor toward the attacker to gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack.

name: Dragon Turtle Plate
description: This harness of +1 resilient half plate is made from the shell plates of a dragon turtle and has the aquadynamic trait. To those who can craft with them, the dense organic plates on the dragon turtle's body are as valuable as the creature's hoard of gold.

name: Dread (Lesser)
description: Eerie symbols cover your armor, inspiring terror in your foes. Frightened enemies within 30 feet that can see you must attempt a DC 20 Will save at the end of their turn; on a failure, the value of their frightened condition doesn't decrease below 1 that turn.

name: Dread Ampoule
description: A vial of fear-inducing alchemical vapor. Deals 1d6 mental and 1 mental splash; on a hit the target is frightened 1 (frightened 2 on a critical hit).

strikes.name: Dread Ampoule
strikes.description: Deals 1d6 mental and 1 mental splash; on a hit the target is frightened 1 (frightened 2 on a critical hit).

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Dread Ampoule (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1d6 mental, 1 splash; frightened 1 (2 on crit)

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Dread Ampoule (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2d6 mental, 2 splash; frightened 1 (2 on crit)

strikes.name: Dread Ampoule
strikes.description: Deals 2d6 mental and 2 mental splash; on a hit the target is frightened 1 (frightened 2 on a critical hit).

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Dread Ampoule (Greater)
variants.effect: 3d6 mental, 3 splash; frightened 1 (2 on crit)

strikes.name: Dread Ampoule
strikes.description: Deals 3d6 mental and 3 mental splash; on a hit the target is frightened 1 (frightened 2 on a critical hit).

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Dread Ampoule (Major)
variants.effect: 4d6 mental, 4 splash; frightened 1 (2 on crit)

strikes.name: Dread Ampoule
strikes.description: Deals 4d6 mental and 4 mental splash; on a hit the target is frightened 1 (frightened 2 on a critical hit).

name: Drums of War
description: The martial cadence of these drums quickens the step of all who march to their beat. While playing the drums, you gain a +1 item bonus to Performance checks and a +5-foot status bonus to your Speed. You can Activate the drums to Cast a Spell from its staff spell list.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Drums of War (Greater)
variants.effect: Adds 2nd- and 3rd-rank spells to the staff.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Drums of War (Major)
variants.effect: The Performance bonus while playing increases to +2 (the +5-foot Speed bonus is unchanged). Adds 4th- and 5th-rank spells to the staff.

name: Dueling Cape
description: A stylish, reinforced cape favored by duelists. With an Interact action you can wrap it around your arm; certain feats and runes rely on the readied cape's defensive bonus.

name: Dweomerweave Robe
description: This robe is made from dweomerweave, a magical fabric created by spinning minor illusions into the threads of ether spider silk. Dweomerweave is naturally hazy and translucent. It can take on the illusory appearances of other fabrics and garments, and dweomerweave robes are coveted by adventurers who want to travel light without compromising on fashion.

actions.name: Change Appearance
actions.description: Activate 1 minute (Interact); Frequency once per day; Effect You gain the effects of a 1st-level illusory disguise, except that the illusion only alters the appearance of the dweomerweave robe, changing it into another garment with an appearance of your choice. The spell persists until the next activation. You can Dismiss the activation.

name: Eagle-eye Elixir

variants.label: Lesser
variants.effect: The bonus is +1, or +2 to find secret doors and traps.

variants.label: Moderate
variants.effect: The bonus is +2, or +3 to find secret doors and traps.

description: After you drink this elixir, you notice subtle visual details. For the next hour, you gain an item bonus to Perception checks that is greater when attempting to find secret doors and traps.

consumable.note: +1 item Perception (1 hr)

name: Elixir of Life

variants.label: Minor
variants.effect: The elixir restores 1d6 Hit Points, and the bonus is +1.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.effect: The elixir restores 3d6+6 Hit Points, and the bonus is +1.

description: Elixirs of life accelerate a living creature natural healing processes and immune system. Upon drinking this elixir, you regain the listed number of Hit Points and gain an item bonus to saving throws against diseases and poisons for 10 minutes.

consumable.note: Restores HP — enter your rolled total

name: Emerald Grasshopper

variants.label: Standard
variants.effect: One action (requires trained in Athletics): make a Leap up to 40 feet horizontally and 10 feet vertically. If you do not end on solid ground, you flutter until the end of your turn, then fall harmlessly at 60 feet per round.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Emerald Grasshopper (Greater)
variants.effect: Your Leap is up to 100 feet horizontally and 30 feet vertically, and applies to any other Leaps you make before the end of your turn.

description: A talisman shaped like a leaping grasshopper, affixed to armor. Activate it to make a prodigious Leap and drift safely to the ground.

consumable.note: Leap 40 ft (100 ft greater); fall harmlessly

name: Energy Robe
description: This brightly colored linen robe is covered in delicate embroidery depicting creatures and natural phenomena suiting its aligned energy, such as a living thunderclap, pools of acid, roaring flames, or dancing marids. The robe grants you resistance to its aligned energy type, and can be activated to call on that energy for a burst of magical mobility.

actions.name: Channel Energy
actions.description: Activate command, Interact; Frequency once per day; Effect You speak a command word and the embroidered threads in the robe glow vividly. The benefit you receive depends on the robe's energy type (see variants).

variants.label: Fire
variants.name: Energy Robe (Fire)
variants.effect: You gain resistance 5 to fire. When the robe is activated, you gain a +30-foot status bonus to Speed for 1 minute as flames shoot out behind you to speed you up.

variants.label: Cold
variants.name: Energy Robe (Cold)
variants.effect: You gain resistance 5 to cold. When the robe is activated, you gain the effects of water walk for 1 minute, as you momentarily flash freeze the surface of water as you walk across it.

variants.label: Acid
variants.name: Energy Robe (Acid)
variants.effect: You gain resistance 5 to acid. When the robe is activated, you gain a climb Speed equal to your Speed, as the acid on your hands grants you easier handholds.

variants.label: Electricity
variants.name: Energy Robe (Electricity)
variants.effect: You gain resistance 5 to electricity. When the robe is activated, you gain a fly Speed equal to your Speed for 1 minute.

name: Entertainer's Lute
description: This finely crafted lute rewards a skilled performer with rich, captivating tones. While playing the lute, you gain a +1 item bonus to Diplomacy and Performance checks. You can Activate the lute to Cast a Spell from its staff spell list.

freeActions.name: Restyle
freeActions.description: Frequency once per round; Effect You play a quick flourish and the look of your hair, clothing, and accessories shifts to a new cosmetic style of your choice. This illusory change lasts as long as you keep the lute in hand and has no mechanical effect.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Entertainer's Lute (Greater)
variants.effect: Adds 2nd- and 3rd-rank spells to the staff.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Entertainer's Lute (Major)
variants.effect: The item bonus while playing increases to +2. Adds 4th- and 5th-rank spells to the staff.

name: Everyneed Pack
description: This green pack is decorated with a white Glyph of the Open Road, and a dozen or more small pockets line the inside. Each pocket is enchanted to contain common, mundane gear worth no more than 1 gp each, such as chalk, flint and steel, or string. It doesn't contain any armor, shields, weapons, or items made of any kind of precious material. Once 8 gp worth of items have been removed, the magic is spent and the pack becomes an ordinary backpack.

actions.name: Draw Supplies
actions.description: Activate 1 minute (Interact); Frequency once per hour; Effect You draw any number of pieces of mundane gear from the pack with a combined value of 1 gp or less.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Everyneed Pack (Greater)
variants.effect: The pockets contain items worth up to 5 gp each. You can draw gear valued at 5 gp or less per activation, and the pack becomes an ordinary backpack after 45 gp worth of items have been removed.

name: Explorer's Clothing
description: Adventurers who don't wear armor travel in durable clothing. Though it's not armor and uses your unarmored defense proficiency, it still has a Dex cap and can be etched with runes.

name: Eye Garnets
description: Large red rubies you pried from a statue of Xar-Azmak.

name: Fan of the Four Winds
description: This ornate fan once belonged to a sage of Gozreh. Activate it (two actions, concentrate, manipulate; once per day) to fan in a direction and cast gust of wind; if directed at a ship's sail, the vessel instead gains a +10-foot circumstance bonus to its Speed for 8 hours. As a relic, the fan can awaken further gifts: feather step and wind barrier (minor), lightning storm and propelling winds (major), and living storm (grand).

name: Fear Gem
description: A talisman set with a smoky gem, affixed to a weapon. Activate it (two actions) to make a melee Strike; if you hit and deal damage, the target is frightened 1, or frightened 2 on a critical hit. If you have the Intimidating Strike feat, increase the frightened value by 1.

consumable.note: Strike → frightened 1 (2 on crit)

name: Clothing (Fine)
description: Fine clothing appropriate for a noble court or formal occasion.

name: Fist Shield
description: A small, sturdy shield strapped across the knuckles and forearm, leaving the hand free. It's a light shield that can be Raised while your hand is empty or holding a light object that isn't a weapon.

name: Flail
description: This weapon consists of a wooden handle attached to a spiked ball or cylinder by a chain, rope, or strap of leather.

name: Flask of Fellowship
description: This metal drinking flask is 4 inches in diameter and 10 inches tall, with a screw top covered by four nested metal cups. When you share drinks as part of a Make an Impression action, the drink that pours out is exactly what the target would most like to have a dram or two of, and you gain a +1 item bonus to that Diplomacy check. The drinks are purely social and provide no nourishment or intoxication. The GM might prevent its use when sharing a drink would be inappropriate.

name: Flint & Steel
description: A camper's classic for starting fires.

name: Frost Vial
description: A vial of supercooled liquid that numbs and slows. Deals 1d6 cold and 1 cold splash; on a hit the target takes a −5-foot status penalty to its Speeds until the end of its next turn.

strikes.name: Frost Vial
strikes.description: Deals 1d6 cold and 1 cold splash; on a hit the target takes a −5-foot status penalty to its Speeds until the end of its next turn.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Frost Vial (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1d6 cold, 1 splash; −5 ft Speed on hit

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Frost Vial (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2d6 cold, 2 splash; −5 ft Speed on hit

strikes.name: Frost Vial
strikes.description: Deals 2d6 cold and 2 cold splash; on a hit the target takes a −5-foot status penalty to its Speeds until the end of its next turn.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Frost Vial (Greater)
variants.effect: 3d6 cold, 3 splash; −5 ft Speed on hit

strikes.name: Frost Vial
strikes.description: Deals 3d6 cold and 3 cold splash; on a hit the target takes a −5-foot status penalty to its Speeds until the end of its next turn.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Frost Vial (Major)
variants.effect: 4d6 cold, 4 splash; −5 ft Speed on hit

strikes.name: Frost Vial
strikes.description: Deals 4d6 cold and 4 cold splash; on a hit the target takes a −5-foot status penalty to its Speeds until the end of its next turn.

name: Full Plate
description: Plate mail consists of interlocking plates that encase nearly the entire body in a carapace of steel. It is costly and heavy, and the wearer often requires help to don it correctly, but it provides some of the best defense armor can supply. A suit of this armor comes with an undercoat of padded armor and a pair of gauntlets.

name: Gate Attenuator

variants.label: Standard
variants.effect: Grants a kineticist a +1 item bonus to impulse attack rolls. Activate (single action, envision) to cast a 1st-rank spell keyed to an element you channel (metal: thunderstrike) with a +7 spell attack modifier or DC 17.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Gate Attenuator (Greater)
variants.effect: The impulse attack bonus increases to +2. Activation casts a 5th-rank element spell (metal: impaling spike) at +18 / DC 28.

description: A crystalline focus worn against the sternum that stabilizes the elemental gate a kineticist channels, sharpening their impulses.

name: Gecko Potion
description: A gecko potion is a sticky, tawny brown liquid with flecks of sand suspended in it. For 5 minutes after drinking this potion, your fingertips sprout thousands of microscopic, bristled hairs that cling to objects, granting you a +1 item bonus to Climb and Palm an Object, and to your Reflex DC against Disarm attempts.

consumable.note: Climb/Palm (5 min)

name: Ghost Charge
description: A charge of positive energy especially harmful to the undead. Deals 1d8 vitality and 1 vitality splash; on a hit the target is enfeebled 1 until the start of your next turn.

strikes.name: Ghost Charge
strikes.description: Deals 1d8 vitality and 1 vitality splash; on a hit the target is enfeebled 1 until the start of your next turn.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Ghost Charge (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1d8 vitality, 1 splash; enfeebled 1 on hit

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Ghost Charge (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2d8 vitality, 2 splash; enfeebled 1 on hit

strikes.name: Ghost Charge
strikes.description: Deals 2d8 vitality and 2 vitality splash; on a hit the target is enfeebled 1 until the start of your next turn.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Ghost Charge (Greater)
variants.effect: 3d8 vitality, 3 splash; enfeebled 1 on hit

strikes.name: Ghost Charge
strikes.description: Deals 3d8 vitality and 3 vitality splash; on a hit the target is enfeebled 1 until the start of your next turn.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Ghost Charge (Major)
variants.effect: 4d8 vitality, 4 splash; enfeebled 1 on hit

strikes.name: Ghost Charge
strikes.description: Deals 4d8 vitality and 4 vitality splash; on a hit the target is enfeebled 1 until the start of your next turn.

actions.name: Recall Name
actions.description: Effect You stare at another creature and instantly remember their name if you've met and exchanged names. The glasses rely on your latent memories, so they don't penetrate a disguised identity.

name: Glasses of Sociability
description: These wire-rim glasses with large, circular lenses grant you a +1 item bonus to Diplomacy checks.

name: Gloom Blade
description: As black as coal, this blade grows more potent in darkness. While in bright light it functions as a +1 shortsword and doesn't radiate a magic aura to detect magic (unless the spell is 4th rank or higher). In dim light or darkness it becomes a +2 striking shortsword. Whenever you attack a creature you're undetected by, you deal 1d6 additional precision damage (descriptive). Toggle the item's illumination mode to match the lighting.

name: Glue Bomb
description: A bomb of fast-setting adhesive that mires its target. Deals no damage. On a hit the target takes a −10-foot status penalty to its Speed for 1 minute; on a critical hit it is immobilized for 1 round (Escape DC 17).

strikes.name: Glue Bomb
strikes.description: Deals no damage. On a hit the target takes a −10-foot status penalty to its Speed for 1 minute; on a critical hit it is immobilized for 1 round (Escape DC 17).

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Glue Bomb (Lesser)
variants.effect: No damage; −10 ft Speed; crit immobilized (Escape DC 17)

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Glue Bomb (Moderate)
variants.effect: No damage; −10 ft Speed; crit immobilized (Escape DC 17)

strikes.name: Glue Bomb
strikes.description: Deals no damage. On a hit the target takes a −10-foot status penalty to its Speed for 1 minute; on a critical hit it is immobilized for 1 round (Escape DC 17).

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Glue Bomb (Greater)
variants.effect: No damage; −15 ft Speed; crit immobilized (Escape DC 28)

strikes.name: Glue Bomb
strikes.description: Deals no damage. On a hit the target takes a −15-foot status penalty to its Speed for 1 minute; on a critical hit it is immobilized for 1 round (Escape DC 28).

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Glue Bomb (Major)
variants.effect: No damage; −20 ft Speed; crit immobilized (Escape DC 37)

strikes.name: Glue Bomb
strikes.description: Deals no damage. On a hit the target takes a −20-foot status penalty to its Speed for 1 minute; on a critical hit it is immobilized for 1 round (Escape DC 37).

name: Goo Grenade
description: A grenade of clinging alchemical goo. Deals 1d4 acid, 1 persistent acid, and 1 acid splash; the clinging goo imposes a −5 circumstance penalty to Speeds (Escape DC 15 or 3 Interact actions).

strikes.name: Goo Grenade
strikes.description: Deals 1d4 acid, 1 persistent acid, and 1 acid splash; the clinging goo imposes a −5 circumstance penalty to Speeds (Escape DC 15 or 3 Interact actions).

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Goo Grenade (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1d4 acid, 1 persistent acid, 1 splash; −5 Speed (Escape DC 15)

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Goo Grenade (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2d4 acid, 2 persistent acid, 2 splash; −5 Speed (Escape DC 15)

strikes.name: Goo Grenade
strikes.description: Deals 2d4 acid, 2 persistent acid, and 2 acid splash; the clinging goo imposes a −5 circumstance penalty to Speeds (Escape DC 15 or 3 Interact actions).

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Goo Grenade (Greater)
variants.effect: 3d4 acid, 3 persistent acid, 3 splash; −5 Speed (Escape DC 15)

strikes.name: Goo Grenade
strikes.description: Deals 3d4 acid, 3 persistent acid, and 3 acid splash; the clinging goo imposes a −5 circumstance penalty to Speeds (Escape DC 15 or 3 Interact actions).

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Goo Grenade (Major)
variants.effect: 4d4 acid, 4 persistent acid, 4 splash; −5 Speed (Escape DC 15)

strikes.name: Goo Grenade
strikes.description: Deals 4d4 acid, 4 persistent acid, and 4 acid splash; the clinging goo imposes a −5 circumstance penalty to Speeds (Escape DC 15 or 3 Interact actions).

name: Gourd Head
description: You have a large gourd for a skull, and since you don't have a physical brain, you can use the space inside your head. You can store a collection of up to 1 Bulk of objects within your head. The DC of checks to Steal objects from inside your head increases by 4. Additionally, if you store only one object within your head, you can draw it effortlessly into your hand as part of another action to use the object. Drawing the item grants this other action the manipulate trait.

name: Greataxe
description: This large battle axe is too heavy to wield with only one hand. Many greataxes incorporate two blades, and they are often "bearded," having a hook at the bottom to increase the strength of their chopping power.

name: Greatsword
description: This massive two-handed sword is nearly 5 feet in length. Its lower blade is often somewhat dulled to allow it to be gripped for extra leverage in close-quarter fights.

reactions.name: Drain Reversal
reactions.trigger: You gain the drained condition from an undead.
reactions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect Reduce the drained value by 1, and the triggering undead takes 2d6 vitality damage.

name: Grim Ring
description: While wearing this ring, you can detect the presence of undead during exploration (without pinpointing locations). The ring can't detect undead whose appearance is masked by an illusion spell of 2nd rank or higher. If an undead is hiding or disguised, the GM rolls a secret Perception check for you against the undead's Stealth or Deception DC, with a +2 item bonus.

name: Halberd
description: This polearm has a relatively short, 5-foot shaft. The business end is a long spike with an axe blade attached. Its reach lets you Strike enemies up to 10 feet away.

name: Half Plate
description: Half plate consists of most of the upper-body plates used in full plate, with lighter or sparser steel plate protection for the arms and legs. This provides some of the protection of full plate with greater flexibility and speed.

name: Handwraps of Mighty Blows
description: These strips of cloth wrap around your hands and wrists. They can have fundamental and property runes etched onto them just like a weapon, and those runes apply to all of your unarmed attacks — your monk's unarmed Strikes included, not only the fist shown here. Upgrade, add, or transfer runes exactly as you would for a weapon; property runes apply only when appropriate to the unarmed attack you're using. Etched here at +1 striking; add a Shock rune later for the electricity theme.

actions.name: Healer's Touch
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect You soothe the wounds of a willing, living, adjacent creature, restoring 2d6+7 Hit Points to that creature. This is a healing vitality effect. You cannot harm undead with this healing.

name: Healer's Gloves
description: These clean, white gloves never show signs of blood, even after they're used to stitch up wounds or treat other ailments. They give you a +1 item bonus to Medicine checks.

name: Healer's Toolkit
description: This kit of bandages, herbs, and suturing tools is necessary for Medicine checks to Administer First Aid, Treat Disease, Treat Poison, or Treat Wounds. If you wear your healer's toolkit, you can draw and replace them as part of the action that uses them.

variants.label: Expanded
variants.name: Healer's Toolkit (Expanded)
variants.effect: Expanded healer's toolkits provide a +1 item bonus to Medicine checks to Administer First Aid, Treat Disease, Treat Poison, or Treat Wounds.

name: Healing Potion

variants.label: Minor
variants.effect: The potion restores 1d8 Hit Points.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.effect: The potion restores 2d8+5 Hit Points.

variants.label: Moderate
variants.effect: The potion restores 3d8+10 Hit Points.

variants.label: Greater
variants.effect: The potion restores 6d8+20 Hit Points.

variants.label: Major
variants.effect: The potion restores 8d8+30 Hit Points.

description: A healing potion is a vial of a ruby-red liquid that imparts a tingling sensation as the drinker's wounds heal rapidly. When you drink a healing potion, you regain the listed number of Hit Points.

consumable.note: Restores HP — enter your rolled total

name: Heater Shield
description: A teardrop-shaped shield sized and balanced for use from horseback. Its riding shield trait suits it to mounted combat.

name: Hide Armor
description: A mix of thick furs and patches of stiff hide, hide armor provides protection due to its layers of leather, although its bulkiness slows the wearer down and decreases mobility.

name: Holy Water
description: This vial contains water blessed by a benevolent deity. You activate a vial of holy water by throwing it as a Strike. It's a simple thrown weapon with a range increment of 20 feet. Holy water deals 1d6 spirit damage and 1 spirit splash damage. Holy water can damage only creatures with the unholy trait.

name: Hoplon
description: A large, deeply concave round shield (an aspis). Its bulk reduces your Speed by 5 feet while you wield it, and it offers no Take Cover benefit.

actions.name: Blow the Horn
actions.description: Once per hour, you can blow on the horn to unleash a swirling aura of jagged, rusty metal shards that orbit you in a 5-foot emanation for 1 minute. The shards grant you a +1 item bonus to AC. Creatures and objects that begin their turn in this area take 6d6 slashing damage from the shards (DC 19 basic Reflex save). A creature damaged by this aura is exposed to a poten form of Tetanus that is a Level 5 disease and has a DC 19 Fortitude Save. Worshippers of Xar-Azmak are immune to this effect.

actions.name: Consume Corruption
actions.description: Once per day, you can place the horn's bell against a diseased creature and then inhale into the mouthpiece rather than blow into it. The horn attempts to draw out the disease, casting Cleanse Affliction with a counteract modifier of +11. If the Horn of Rust succeeds at countering the disease, it gains a number of staff charges equal to half the disease's level rounded down. If it critically fails, not only is the disease not countered, but you are exposed to the disease and must attempt a saving throw against it to resist contracting the affliction yourself.

name: Horn of Rust
description: This rusted, coiled iron horn is sizable, appearing similar in shape to a ram's horn but measuring nearly 2 feet in diameter. Flakes of rust constantly fall from it, and the item leaves hands with a rusty red stain that lingers. While the Horn of Rust's power has diminished over the ages since Xar-Azmak's death, those who carry it are still subject to its palpable chaotic influences. If you are not sanctified Unholy, you are Enfeebled 1 while carrying or using the Horn of Rust; if you are sanctified Holy, you are instead Enfeebled 2 and Stupefied 1. It can be prepared as a staff, granting four spells.

name: Hunter's Bow
description: This +1 shortbow is favored by stealthy hunters and rogues. If you Strike a target that can't see you and score a critical hit, the target takes an extra 1d6 damage (in addition to any sneak attack damage).

name: Ichthyosis Mutagen
description: Your skin thickens into overlapping scales that knit wounds closed and seal bleeding, but flake constantly and slow your reflexes — leaving an easy trail to follow.

consumable.note: Fast healing 2, auto-recover persistent bleed; −2 Reflex; trackable +4 for 24 hr (1 min)

actions.name: Cast Thoughtful Gift
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Range 120 feet; Targets 1 willing creature. You teleport one object of light or negligible Bulk held in your hand to the target. The object appears in the target's hand if they have a free hand, or at their feet if they don't. The spell fails if the target is unconscious, refuses your gift, or if the spell would teleport a creature.

name: Idolis Tattoo
description: This tattoo features traditional Varisian geometry and is part of an astrological set of symbol tattoos. Having one provides you with a +1 to Diplomacy checks when speaking to native Varisians, and allows you to cast Thoughtful Gift once per day. (Usage: tattooed on the body.)

name: Inventor's Chair
description: A traveler's chair crafted by a gifted inventor who lost the use of their legs, it is a marvel of clockwork engineering that maneuvers about on its own, equipped with wheel blades and built-in sterling artisan's tools. As a relic it awakens further gifts: emotion surge and a Crafting muse (minor), emotion conduit and enthralling (major), and artistic perfection in Crafting (grand).

name: Jade Cat
description: A talisman carved from jade in the shape of a cat, affixed to armor. Activate it (free action) when you fall or attempt an Acrobatics check to Balance (requires trained in Acrobatics). For 1 minute, you treat all falls as 20 feet shorter, you are not off-guard when you Balance, and narrow surfaces and uneven ground are not difficult terrain for you.

consumable.note: Falls 20 ft shorter; sure-footed Balance (1 min)

name: Juggernaut Mutagen
description: An alchemical mutagen that toughens the body, granting resilience and temporary vigor.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Juggernaut Mutagen (Lesser)
variants.effect: The bonus is +1 and you gain 5 temporary HP.

consumable.note: +1 Fort, 5 temp HP; −2 Will, Perception, initiative (1 min)

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Juggernaut Mutagen (Moderate)
variants.effect: The bonus is +2 and you gain 10 temporary HP.

consumable.note: +2 Fort, 10 temp HP; −2 Will, Perception, initiative (10 min)

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Juggernaut Mutagen (Greater)
variants.effect: The bonus is +3 and you gain 30 temporary HP.

consumable.note: +3 Fort, 30 temp HP; −2 Will, Perception, initiative (1 hr)

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Juggernaut Mutagen (Major)
variants.effect: The bonus is +4 and you gain 45 temporary HP.

consumable.note: +4 Fort, 45 temp HP; −2 Will, Perception, initiative (1 hr)

name: Katana
description: A curved, single-edged sword known for its wickedly sharp blade.

name: Kite Shield
description: A tall shield tapering to a point, angled to turn aside missiles. Its deflecting trait grants +2 Hardness when you Shield Block a ranged attack.

name: Kukri
description: The blade of this foot-long knife curves inward and lacks a cross guard at the hilt.

name: Lady's Knife
description: This +1 striking returning dagger has an elaborate, gemmed handle and can be strapped to the inside of a wrist or tucked within a bodice.

actions.name: Favor
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect You grant the blade to an ally, forming a link that lasts until your next daily preparations. While linked, you and your ally each gain the benefits of the status spell, using each other as the target.

actions.name: Recall
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Requirements You have an active Favor link; Effect The knife teleports instantly to your hand. This fails if the knife is more than 1 mile away. Your linked ally can also use this activation once per day to recall the knife.

name: Leather Armor
description: A mix of flexible and molded boiled leather, a suit of this type of armor provides some protection with maximum flexibility.

name: Longbow
description: This 5-foot-tall bow, usually made of a single piece of elm, hickory, or yew, has a powerful draw and is excellent at propelling arrows with great force and at an extreme distance. You must use two hands to fire a longbow, and it can't be used while mounted. Because it's made for long range, you take a -2 penalty to attacks on creatures within 30ft with it.

name: Longsword
description: Longswords can be one-edged or two-edged swords. Their blades are heavy and they're between 3 and 4 feet in length.

name: Mace
description: With a stout haft and a heavy metal head, a mace is sturdy and reliable. Its weight and balance make it well suited to Shoving enemies off balance.

name: Magic Scroll
description: A scroll is a spell stored in written form. Casting the spell from a scroll requires Activating it (Cast a Spell), after which the scroll is consumed. A specific scroll is named for the spell it holds (Scroll of Heal, Scroll of Web), and derives its rank, item level, price, and Bulk from that spell. This base entry carries the shared scroll artwork that every such scroll displays.

name: Magic Wand
description: A magic wand holds a single spell, which you can cast once per day by Activating it (Cast a Spell), with the option to overcharge it for an additional casting at some risk. A specific wand is named for the spell it holds (Wand of Heal), and derives its rank, item level, price, and Bulk from that spell. This base entry carries the shared wand artwork that every such wand displays.

name: Masquerade Scarf
description: This delicately embroidered scarf seems to match every outfit. Activate it (1 minute, manipulate; once per day) to arrange it over your lower face and cast a 1st-rank illusory disguise on yourself; the disguise ends immediately if the scarf is removed. The scarf's own appearance can be altered or made invisible, though it remains detectable by touch. (A greater version, item 7, activates in 2 actions any number of times per day and casts a 2nd-rank illusory disguise.)

name: Maul
description: Mauls are massive warhammers that must be swung with two hands.

name: Mesmerizing Opal

activation.trigger: You activate the opal and attempt a Deception check to Feint

description: This silver-bound opal pendant is afire with iridescence. When you activate it, attempt a Deception check to Feint. If the outcome is a success, you get a critical success instead; if it is a critical failure, you get a failure instead.

name: Midnight Milk
description: The strange, swirling liquid in this bottle is said to give those who injest it a feeling of drowsy euphoria as it lulls them to sleep. It is a new drug, making its way into surface markets via trade routes in the Darklands.

name: Mirror Robe
description: Thousands of small, reflective, mirrored glass shards have been carefully stitched down this long silk duster.

actions.name: Mirrored Diversion
actions.description: Activate Interact; Requirements The mirror robe was last activated to draw attention toward you, or you haven't activated the mirror robe today; Effect The mirror robe Creates a Diversion for you, using a +9 modifier for the Deception check.

actions.name: Draw Attention
actions.description: Activate Interact; Requirements The mirror robe was last activated to divert attention away from you, and you are hidden from or undetected by at least one foe; Effect You choose one foe to whom you were hidden or undetected, reveal yourself to all creatures (becoming observed), and that foe becomes off-guard to your allies until the beginning of your next turn.

name: Mistform Elixir

variants.label: Lesser
variants.effect: A faint mist makes you concealed for 3 rounds.

variants.label: Moderate
variants.effect: A faint mist makes you concealed for 1 minute.

variants.label: Greater
variants.effect: A faint mist makes you concealed for 5 minutes.

description: Upon drinking this elixir, a faint mist emanates from your skin, making you concealed for the listed duration. Concealment does not let you Hide if your position is otherwise obvious.

consumable.note: Concealed (3 rounds / 1 min / 5 min)

name: Moderate Drakeheart Mutagen
description: Your skin grows scales like a drake, and your eyesight become sharp and your pupils slitted, but your mind and reflexes slow. You gain a +5 Item bonus to AC, a Dexterity cap of +2 (as usual, use your lowest Dexterity cap if you have more than one), and a +2 item bonus to Perception checks. If you're wearing armor, you still calculate your proficiency bonus to AC based on your proficiency in the armor you're wearing, even if the drakeheart mutagen has a higher item bonus. While under its effects, you take a –1 penalty to Will saves, Reflex saves, and all skill checks to Recall Knowledge. As a single action, you can burn through the remaining duration of the Mutagen to stride twice.

consumable.note: +5 item AC, +2 item Perception; Dex cap +2 & −1 Will/Reflex/RK penalties (see effect)

name: Monarch
description: Monarch functions as a +1 striking returning starknife that can't have additional property runes, as it's a specific item. Monarch deals an additional 1d6 untyped damage to creatures with the aberration trait, provided the creature isn't disguised as another creature (this rider is descriptive — apply it by hand vs. aberrations). Monarch is a relic and has no market price.

actions.name: Dream Message
actions.description: Activate (10 minutes, envision, Interact); Frequency once per day; Requirements Monarch possesses a major gift; Effect Monarch casts dream message (heightened to 4th rank) to your specifications.

name: Moonlit Chain
description: This +1 silver chain shirt has a collar adorned with stitched images of the phases of the moon. You can see in moonlight as though you had low-light vision.

actions.name: Dim Sight
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect You touch the stitched image of the new moon on the armor's collar and suppress the dazzled condition for 1 minute.

reactions.name: Amulet's Abeyance
reactions.trigger: The target of your Exploit Vulnerability would damage you or an ally within 15 feet of you and you are holding your implement.
reactions.description: You forcefully present your amulet to turn away harm. You or a target ally within 15 feet gain resistance to all damage against the triggering damage. The resistance is equal to 2 + your level.

name: Mother's Amulet
description: A blood-red ruby set in irridescent metal, the last gift your mother gave you.

name: Mud Bomb
description: A bomb of dense clinging mud. Deals 1d6 bludgeoning and 1 bludgeoning splash; on a critical hit the target is dazzled until the start of your next turn.

strikes.name: Mud Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 1d6 bludgeoning and 1 bludgeoning splash; on a critical hit the target is dazzled until the start of your next turn.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Mud Bomb (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1d6 bludgeoning, 1 splash; crit dazzled

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Mud Bomb (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2d6 bludgeoning, 2 splash; crit dazzled

strikes.name: Mud Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 2d6 bludgeoning and 2 bludgeoning splash; on a critical hit the target is dazzled until the start of your next turn.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Mud Bomb (Greater)
variants.effect: 3d6 bludgeoning, 3 splash; crit dazzled

strikes.name: Mud Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 3d6 bludgeoning and 3 bludgeoning splash; on a critical hit the target is dazzled until the start of your next turn.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Mud Bomb (Major)
variants.effect: 4d6 bludgeoning, 4 splash; crit dazzled

strikes.name: Mud Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 4d6 bludgeoning and 4 bludgeoning splash; on a critical hit the target is dazzled until the start of your next turn.

name: Mysterious Blue Orb
description: Streaks of blue shimmer within the smoky depths of this perfect crystalline sphere. Both the scintillating shade and emanations of an indefinable power suggest that something slumbers within. When all is quiet, Blu can feel it pulling at him.

name: Necrotic Bomb
description: A bomb of life-draining negative energy. Deals 1d6 void and 1 void splash; on a critical hit the target is sickened 1.

strikes.name: Necrotic Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 1d6 void and 1 void splash; on a critical hit the target is sickened 1.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Necrotic Bomb (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1d6 void, 1 splash; crit sickened 1

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Necrotic Bomb (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2d6 void, 2 splash; crit sickened 1

strikes.name: Necrotic Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 2d6 void and 2 void splash; on a critical hit the target is sickened 1.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Necrotic Bomb (Greater)
variants.effect: 3d6 void, 3 splash; crit sickened 1

strikes.name: Necrotic Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 3d6 void and 3 void splash; on a critical hit the target is sickened 1.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Necrotic Bomb (Major)
variants.effect: 4d6 void, 4 splash; crit sickened 1

strikes.name: Necrotic Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 4d6 void and 4 void splash; on a critical hit the target is sickened 1.

name: Obsidian Goggles
description: These sleek goggles have black lenses that make everything seem more brightly lit, granting a +1 item bonus to Perception checks involving sight. Activate them (single action, manipulate; once per day) to gain darkvision for 1 hour. (Greater, item 11, gives a +2 bonus and unlimited use; major, item 18, gives +3 and greater darkvision.)

name: Oil of Weightlessness
description: You can spread this shimmering oil on an item of 1 Bulk or less to make it feel weightless. It has negligible Bulk for 1 hour.

consumable.label: Weightless
consumable.note: Negligible Bulk (1 hr)

name: Ommatophoric Mutagen
description: Upon activation your eyes bulge from their sockets and extend upward on long, prehensile stalks, greatly enhancing your eyesight and field of view but leaving you unable to close them.

consumable.note: +2 visual Perception, all-around vision (unflankable), low-light; −2 saves vs visual (1 min)

name: Clothing (Ordinary)
description: Ordinary, everyday clothing.

name: Padded Armor
description: This armor is simply a layer of heavy, quilted cloth, but it is sometimes used because it's so inexpensive. Padded armor is easier to damage and destroy than other types of armor.
